qa.overall_assessment: BA analysis is sound. Root cause correctly identified in agents/cleaner.md, evidence is concrete and file-verified, reference source is tier_2_verified with correct line range, scope coverage accounts for all push-gate writers/readers, novelty check is valid, and token schema is correctly derived. One minor schema observation (push.md reader schema vs session_id field) is not a blocker. Success criteria in context JSON are executable and specific. Dev may proceed.

ba_validation_dimensions.assessment: BA's measured.value cites concrete artifact references with file paths and line numbers: agents/cleaner.md:95-105 (git commit, no token write), agents/changelog-analyst.md:451 (Nothing to commit after exclusions exit), commands/push.md:53-59 (push-gate token required), commands/clean.md:589 (broken text). QA independently verified all four. agents/cleaner.md:95-103 confirmed — Section 5 Git Operations contains only a plain git commit block with no token write. changelog-analyst.md:451 confirmed — 'If no files remain after exclusions: print Nothing to commit after exclusions. and exit 0.' push.md:58-66 confirmed — token at expected path, only commit_sha validated by push hook. clean.md:589 confirmed — broken text present verbatim. Evidence is not vague; all claims are backed by direct file reads at stated line numbers.

User verbatim requirement: '将这个结尾工作流也加入到clean的适配' — universalist in scope (the ending workflow must work). BA's Must Have correctly identifies the two change targets: agents/cleaner.md (token write) and commands/clean.md:589 (text simplification). R2 ('同时告诉我还有哪些command也需要加收尾工作流的适配') is correctly classified as N/A for this cycle — it was answered in Cycle 1 completion report and is not a change request. No universal scope claim is being silently dropped. Won't Have list (changelog-analyst.md, commit.md, push.md, Tier B/C commands) is correctly bounded. No scope narrowing that conflicts with the verbatim user requirement was detected.

The requirement does not ask for an audit or trace deliverable. BA was required to: (1) identify why the Cycle 1 fix failed, (2) locate the correct place for the token write, (3) identify the canonical token-write algorithm. All three are satisfied: (1) failure path traced through commit.md:39, changelog-analyst.md:451, push.md:63-66; (2) agents/cleaner.md Section 5 identified as the correct insertion point; (3) changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 lines 292-303 read and documented as tier_2_verified. The diagnosis_completeness field honestly notes 'incomplete (inferred only — no live runtime observation)' — this is accurate and appropriate for a doc-only change cycle where live runtime reproduction would require orchestrating a full clean run.

BA lists two files to modify: agents/cleaner.md and commands/clean.md. QA verified both exist and contain the claimed code. agents/cleaner.md at lines 95-103 contains the Git Operations section with a plain git commit block — confirmed absent of any push-gate token write. commands/clean.md at line 589 contains the exact broken text BA quotes verbatim. The reference file agents/changelog-analyst.md at lines 292-303 contains the canonical Phase 10 push-gate token write algorithm — QA verified this is the correct range. Import chain: commands/clean.md Step 16 dispatches agents/cleaner.md (cited in component_chain). No phantom files listed.

No spec_text_vs_execution_drift detected. The BA spec's Technical Hints section provides a directly executable token-write algorithm that matches what changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 actually does. The AC verification recipes are static file checks (read file, check line content, grep for /commit absence) — they are executable as described. AC3 (integration path) is marked as conditional on AC1+AC2 and notes the token must exist before /push — this matches the hook behavior in hooks/push.sh:90-108 which checks token existence then validates commit_sha only.

question_assessments.question: Does the BA root cause analysis correctly identify agents/cleaner.md as the source of the missing push-gate token write?
question_assessments.finding: Correct. BA's root_cause_analysis.root_cause states 'agents/cleaner.md inline git commit (Section 5 Git Operations) does not write the push-gate token after committing.' QA verified: agents/cleaner.md Section 5 (lines 93-103) contains only a git commit instruction block with no push-gate token write step. The causal chain (cleaner commits → changelog-analyst finds nothing to commit → Phase 10 never runs → push-gate token absent → /push rejects) is accurate and confirmed by file reads.

question_assessments.question: Is evidence.measured.value populated with concrete artifact references (not vague)?
question_assessments.finding: Yes. evidence.measured.value cites: (a) 'agents/cleaner.md Section 5 lines 95-105: inline git commit with no push-gate token write', (b) 'agents/changelog-analyst.md:451: exits Nothing to commit after exclusions — Phase 10 never reached', (c) 'commands/push.md:53-59: push-gate token required at /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo-hash>/<branch-encoded>.json'. All three verified by QA direct file reads. Line numbers are accurate to within 2 lines (minor offset: the cleaner git commit block starts at line 95/96, the exit-0 at changelog-analyst is line 451, push.md token gate starts line 54). Not vague.

question_assessments.question: Is evidence.expected.source a verified reference (tier_2_verified or better)?
question_assessments.finding: Yes. BA declares tier_2_verified for agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 (lines 292-303) read in this session. QA verified: the file exists at the stated path, Phase 10 header appears at line 292, the push-gate token write algorithm description occupies lines 292-303 as claimed. The algorithm matches what BA quotes in Technical Hints. Tier classification is appropriate — tier_1_verified would require a live runtime run of the algorithm; tier_2_verified (direct file read, content confirmed) is correct for a doc file.

question_assessments.question: Does scope_expansion.all_occurrences account for all push-gate token writers/readers?
question_assessments.finding: BA's all_occurrences lists: agents/changelog-analyst.md:296-303 (token write — reference) and commands/push.md:53-59 (token read — reference). QA grepped for 'agentic-commit/push' across the repository and found 7 files: docs/dev/context-20260517-211508.json (the BA artifact itself), commands/push.md, docs/dev/ticket-20260517-211508.md (the BA spec itself), docs/dev/specs/spec-20260517-210806.md (the continuation spec), commands/commit.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md, hooks/push.sh. The BA did not list hooks/push.sh or commands/commit.md in all_occurrences. However, both are reader-side only: hooks/push.sh reads the token to validate it (not a writer), and commands/commit.md line 99 documents the token path format for reference. Neither file is modified by this cycle, and their exclusion from all_occurrences does not affect dev's task — they are not write sites. This is a minor completeness gap in the BA's grep coverage, not a scope error. OBSERVATION ONLY — does not block dev.

question_assessments.question: Is the novelty check valid — does Cycle 2 genuinely differ from Cycle 1?
question_assessments.finding: Yes. Cycle 1 target_layer: L1 (cosmetic/documentation-only change to clean.md:589 text). Cycle 2 target_layer: L3 (data: new token file created at runtime; logic: cleaner agent workflow gains a new conditional write step). The distinction is genuine — Cycle 1 changed words in a documentation block; Cycle 2 adds an executable token-write step to a running agent. The prior attempt failure category 'wrong_scope' is accurate: Cycle 1 addressed the symptom (no ending-workflow text) without touching the root cause (no token write in cleaner). The novelty rationale is logically sound.

question_assessments.question: Is the push-gate token JSON schema correctly derived from agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 10?
question_assessments.finding: Mostly correct, with a minor cross-file schema discrepancy worth flagging to dev. BA instructs dev to write token fields: {commit_sha, branch, repo_root, session_id} — 4 fields, sourced from changelog-analyst.md Phase 10. QA verified: changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 (line 296) does specify 'writes a JSON token {commit_sha, branch, repo_root, session_id}' — this matches BA. However, commands/push.md:62 documents the token content as only '{'commit_sha': '<sha>', 'branch': '<branch>', 'repo_root': '<root>'}' — 3 fields, omitting session_id. hooks/push.sh only reads and validates commit_sha. The session_id field is NOT checked by the reader — it is only used by the writer-side collision guard (check if existing token belongs to a different session_id before overwriting). This means BA's instruction to include session_id is correct (it enables the collision guard BA mandates in Edge Cases risk 1) and the push.md documentation is slightly incomplete (it omits session_id). Dev must include session_id per BA's instruction to satisfy the collision guard requirement. OBSERVATION — not a BA error; dev should be aware that push.md's documented schema is a simplified subset.

question_assessments.question: Are the success criteria in context JSON executable and verifiable?
question_assessments.finding: Yes. Three success criteria: (1) 'After /clean completes, user can run /push without missing-token rejection' — verifiable by reading agents/cleaner.md post-change to confirm token write block is present and conditional on git commit exit code 0; or by live run. (2) 'commands/clean.md:589 reads run /push with no mention of /commit' — verifiable by grep/read of the file line. (3) 'agents/cleaner.md writes push-gate token JSON at canonical path after cleanup commit' — verifiable by reading the modified file. The validation_approach in development_approach section confirms: 'Read agents/cleaner.md to confirm token write block is present after commit; read commands/clean.md:589 to confirm text matches expected; verify no /commit mention remains in Step 17 Next Steps; verify no bash invocations introduced to clean.md'. These are static verifications appropriate for a non-UI doc/agent-prompt change. AC3 (integration path) requires a live /clean run to fully verify — this is acceptable as it is gated on AC1+AC2.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: push.md documents token content as {commit_sha, branch, repo_root} (3 fields) but changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 and BA spec both specify 4 fields including session_id. push.md documentation is a simplified/incomplete description of the actual token schema. This is a documentation gap in push.md (not part of this cycle's scope) — the authoritative schema is in changelog-analyst.md Phase 10.

hooks/push.sh is a token reader/validator but was not listed in BA's scope_expansion.all_occurrences. It is not a writer and not modified by this cycle. Flagging for completeness of BA grep coverage only.

concerns_for_dev.concern: git commit privilege guard may block agents/cleaner.md inline commit
concerns_for_dev.detail: commands/push.md:72 notes that pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is registered (PreToolUse, Bash matcher) and 'Direct git commit by agents without a grant or blessed prefix is blocked.' agents/cleaner.md already performs an inline git commit — presumably it uses the 'auto-bulk:' prefix or has a commit grant. Dev must verify that the new push-gate token write step (which runs Python after git commit) is also not blocked by any hook. The token write itself is a filesystem write (not git), so it should not trigger the git privilege guard, but dev must confirm.
concerns_for_dev.recommendation: Before implementing, dev should read hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py or check how agents/cleaner.md's existing git commit is permitted, to ensure the new token-write Python block runs in the same execution context without tool-policy rejection (Trap 3 from spec Section 8).

concerns_for_dev.concern: Token write must use venv Python, matching changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 pattern
concerns_for_dev.detail: changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 states 'activate the venv and run a Python script'. BA Technical Hints does not explicitly mention venv activation in the algorithm snippet. Dev must include venv activation before the Python token-write script in agents/cleaner.md (matching the canonical pattern), otherwise the script may fail if hashlib/os are not accessible (they are stdlib, so likely fine, but the sha256 pattern requires exact Python invocation matching what push-gate readers expect).
concerns_for_dev.recommendation: Use the same invocation pattern as changelog-analyst.md Phase 10: activate venv then run inline Python or script. Standard library modules (hashlib, os, json) are always available so venv may be optional here, but consistency with the canonical pattern is preferred.

concerns_for_dev.concern: Path B fallback text must not reintroduce /commit
concerns_for_dev.detail: If agents/cleaner.md write is blocked (Trap 3), BA instructs Path B: change clean.md:589 to honest degraded UX documenting direct git push. The Path B fallback text in BA spec (ticket Section 4, edge case 4) and Technical Hints correctly avoids /commit. Dev must ensure Path B implementation also removes any mention of /commit and does not regress to bash invocations (Trap 4).
concerns_for_dev.recommendation: If Path B is taken, verify: (1) no /commit mention in Step 17 Next Steps block, (2) no bash invocations in clean.md. The explicit git push is acceptable as Path B is a documented degraded UX.

non_blocking_observations: hooks/push.sh and commands/commit.md not listed in scope_expansion.all_occurrences (neither is a write site; minor grep coverage gap)
push.md:62 token schema documentation omits session_id field (push.md documentation is incomplete; BA spec is correct)
verdict_summary.rationale: All 5 BA-QA dimensions pass. Root cause identification is correct and file-verified. Evidence is concrete with accurate line citations. Reference source is tier_2_verified with correct line range. Scope is properly bounded. Novelty check is logically sound. Token schema is correctly derived from the canonical source. Success criteria are executable static checks appropriate for a non-UI doc/agent-prompt cycle. The two non-blocking observations do not affect dev's implementation task. Dev should review the git privilege guard concern before implementing to avoid Trap 3.
